sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 94

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 94:1
items.html:
(to the question) Kista de Chayuta
When the Partzuf rises as MAN, it remains in its place with the departure of the Mochin; and then it is discerned that there remains in it a discernment of Kista de Chayuta, meaning a Reshimo [record] of its Mochin, which is its discernment of subsistence. It is roughly like a sleeping man: with the departure of all the powers from him, he is nonetheless not deemed dead, but there is a little vitality in him, such that he is fit to have all his vitality return to him when he stirs from his sleep. So this Reshimo, called Kista de Chayuta, is what receives back all his Mochin at the time when the Mochin return to him from the upper one. (p. 810, Item 108)
